metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete predicate `latest_ticket_digest` uses to decide 'ticket is gone' for lazy prune (e.g., `state.get('status') in ('archived','deleted')` after a successful `show_state`, OR `ReadError` for deleted/tombstoned tickets), and confirm `test_prune_archived` exercises the archived-status path explicitly.
findings.finding: The lazy prune trigger 'digest whose ticket is archived/deleted is dropped lazily when `latest_ticket_digest` observes the ticket is gone' is mechanically unsound for archived tickets: `show_state` does NOT raise ReadError for tickets with status 'archived' — archived tickets still have their directory and events present. The natural implementation of 'ticket is gone' (catching ReadError from show_state) would never fire for archived tickets, only for truly absent/deleted ones, making `test_prune_archived` unreachable via this mechanism.
findings.location: Prune invocation section / `latest_ticket_digest` description / AC: `::test_prune_archived`
findings.suggested_fix: In `latest_ticket_digest`, after loading the sidecar, call `show_state` and check `state.get('status') in ('archived', 'deleted')` (or check for `.tombstone.json` / `.archived` marker directly) rather than relying on ReadError alone; document the predicate in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the archive/deletion detection mechanism in `latest_ticket_digest` (e.g. catch `ReadError` from `show_state`, check ticket `status == 'archived'`, or detect a missing/empty ticket directory) and document whether it mirrors the best-effort posture of the rest of the sidecar.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel lazy-deletion behaviour — 'a digest whose ticket is archived/deleted is dropped lazily when `latest_ticket_digest` observes the ticket is gone' — that has no counterpart in the `REVIEW_RESULT` prior art (`sidecar.py` has no such detection). The mechanism for detecting 'ticket is gone' is unspecified: does `latest_ticket_digest` call `show_state`, inspect for an ARCHIVED event, or check directory existence? Without this, `test_prune_archived` has no grounded implementation target.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prune invocation' paragraph; AC: `::test_prune_archived`; compare with `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` (no archived-ticket detection)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the lazy archive-prune protocol in `latest_ticket_digest`: how 'gone' is detected, whether the drop is git-backed or best-effort unlink, and what happens on drop failure (suggest: best-effort, swallowed, returns None regardless).
findings.finding: The 'lazy archive prune' decision rule — 'a digest whose ticket is archived/deleted is dropped lazily when `latest_ticket_digest` observes the ticket is gone' — is referenced but its execution protocol is unspecified: (a) how is 'ticket is gone' detected (ReadError from show_state? status check?), (b) is the drop a git-rm+commit (like `prune`) or a filesystem unlink, and (c) does a failure in the lazy drop suppress the None return or is it best-effort?
findings.location: Plan section: 'Prune invocation' / `test_prune_archived` AC item

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the 'S5/`ee3d`' hedge: either identify the correct story ID for S5 (if it is a different story than S1/ee3d) or remove the hedge and state unconditionally that this story owns the `ticket_text` promotion.
findings.finding: The plan's conditional hedge says 'If S5/`ee3d`, lower in the stack, has already promoted `ticket_text`, this story simply imports it and does not re-promote.' The alias `indecisive-callous-armyworm` maps to `ee3d`, which is listed as S1 (not S5) in the dependency line 'S1 (indecisive-callous-armyworm)'. S1/ee3d's ticket text confirms it uses `gates._ticket_text` directly and does NOT promote it. This mislabeling creates a false scope-exclusion hedge: the condition 'S5/ee3d has already promoted' can never fire as written, potentially confusing implementers about which story owns the promotion.
findings.location: Section 'Content hash — REUSE the canonical normalizer', last parenthetical: 'If S5/`ee3d`, lower in the stack, has already promoted `ticket_text`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line covering `latest_ticket_digest` HLC-ordered selection (e.g., 'latest_ticket_digest returns the most-recent-by-HLC append when multiple TICKET_DIGEST events exist; `::test_latest_wins`') and add one sentence to the Scope section specifying that `latest_ticket_digest` selects by HLC descending.
findings.finding: `test_latest_wins` (two appends, latest-by-HLC) appears in the Testing section but no plan-body section describes HOW `latest_ticket_digest` selects the latest record (HLC ordering), and no AC line covers this behavior. This is an uncovered deliverable (the tie-breaking/selection algorithm) with an orphan test.
findings.location: Testing section (`test_latest_wins`) vs Scope section (`latest_ticket_digest`) vs Acceptance Criteria (no matching criterion)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle the plan (or add a clarifying note) to reflect that `content_hash` is a **freshness tag inside the payload**, not the storage key — e.g. 'Persist ticket digest as a content-hash-versioned sidecar (shared cache)' — so implementers are not misled into a content-addressed upsert model.
findings.finding: The title 'content-hash-keyed record' implies the content_hash IS the storage key (content-addressable lookup), but the body stores events keyed by HLC timestamp with content_hash as a freshness tag inside the payload; lookup is by ticket_id+sentinel, not by hash. An implementer reading the title literally could build an upsert-by-hash model instead of append+prune.
findings.location: Plan title and 'Freshness (computed on read)' section
findings.suggested_fix: Change the title to: 'Persist ticket digest as a content-hash-versioned sidecar (shared cache)' and optionally add one sentence in the 'Content hash' section: 'Note: content_hash is stored as a payload field for freshness validation, not as the event's storage key (which is the HLC timestamp).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `latest_ticket_digest` walk-backs over corrupt or wrong-sentinel files (matching `latest_review_result`'s behavior) or returns `None` on the first non-v1 file, and add a test case for the wrong-sentinel scenario.
findings.finding: The `latest_ticket_digest` read function's behavior on a corrupt newest file or a file with a non-`ticket_digest_v1` schema sentinel is under-specified. The plan says it 'guards on the `ticket_digest_v1` sentinel' but does not state whether it walk-backs to older files (as `latest_review_result` does in sidecar.py) or returns `None` immediately. This is a processing protocol co-located with the schema guard that `test_fail_closed` partially covers but `test_roundtrip` / `test_latest_wins` do not.
findings.location: Plan section: 'New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/digest_sidecar.py`' / `latest_ticket_digest(ticket_id)` description
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `latest_ticket_digest` description: 'Walk-back: a file that is unreadable (OSError/ValueError) or whose schema != "ticket_digest_v1" is skipped with a logger.warning; the reader returns the first usable v1 payload, or None if all files are unusable — matching the latest_review_result walk-back contract in plan_review/sidecar.py.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the AC's `test_hash_parity` bullet to reference `ticket_text` (the promoted public name) instead of `_ticket_text`, keeping it consistent with the Scope's promotion spec.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section says this story promotes `_ticket_text` to a public `ticket_text()`, but the AC line still reads 'content_hash uses `_ticket_text` (parity with S1)' — referencing the private name that this very story is supposed to rename/alias. If a developer implements the promotion as specified, the test `test_hash_parity` should import/use `ticket_text` (the new public name), not `_ticket_text`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, second bullet: 'content_hash uses `_ticket_text` (parity with S1)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'content_hash uses `_ticket_text` (parity with S1)' to 'content_hash uses the promoted public `ticket_text` (parity with S1); `::test_hash_parity` imports `ticket_text` from `gates`, not the private alias'.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `test_hash_parity` reference in the AC: replace 'parity with S1' with 'parity with S5' (or whichever story actually shares the `ticket_text` call path with this sidecar) so the AC and Testing section agree on what the test asserts.
findings.finding: The AC says `test_hash_parity` verifies 'parity with S1', but the Testing section says `test_hash_parity` checks that 'this ticket's `content_hash` == S5's over shared fixtures'. S1 produces the digest payload; S5 is the enrich story that also calls `ticket_text`. These are different stories, and the test assertion target (S5) contradicts the AC citation (S1).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria bullet 2 vs Testing section `test_hash_parity` entry
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC bullet 2 parenthetical from '(parity with S1)' to '(parity with S5/enrich)' to match the Testing section description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the `_cmd_show` freshness render spec whether `digest_freshness` should appear in the `--output llm` arm (i.e., whether the injection happens before or after the `to_llm(state)` call, or whether `to_llm` needs a skip rule for this key).
findings.finding: The plan specifies injecting `digest_freshness` into the `state` dict inside `_cmd_show` and then passing `state` to `to_llm(state)` for the `--output llm` arm. The actual `to_llm()` at `src/rebar/reducer/llm_format.py:142-163` iterates ALL keys in its input dict and emits those not in `OMIT_KEYS` — so an injected `digest_freshness` key would flow through verbatim into the LLM output shape. The plan says `digest_freshness` is a 'CLI-presentation-only field that never enters the library show_ticket/list/search returns,' but it does not address whether the `--output llm` arm of `show` should also carry this field (or whether `to_llm` needs a skip rule for it).
findings.location: Scope section / `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py::_cmd_show` / `show` freshness render description

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Testing section whether `test_hash_parity` defines its own local fixtures (preferred) or imports from S5, and if local, state that explicitly; add S5 as a declared dependency only if the test requires S5's code.
findings.finding: test_hash_parity's proving command depends on 'shared fixtures' with S5 (enrich) but S5 is not listed as a dependency, leaving it ambiguous whether the test imports S5's actual functions (requiring S5 to be complete) or merely uses fixture data that will also be reused by S5. If the former, the test cannot be written without S5, making this a hidden unresolved dependency; if the latter, the plan should state the fixtures are defined locally in this story's test file.
findings.location: Testing section: `test_hash_parity` description; Dependencies section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the test_hash_parity description: 'Fixtures (sample ticket title/description/comments) are defined locally in this test file and will be reused by S5's tests — no import of S5 code required.' This removes the ambiguity and keeps the story self-contained.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update the AC bullet 2 parenthetical from 'content_hash uses `_ticket_text`' to 'content_hash uses `ticket_text`' to match the promoted public name used throughout the rest of the plan.
findings.finding: The AC references the private symbol `_ticket_text` ('content_hash uses `_ticket_text`') while the entire plan body is dedicated to promoting it to the public name `ticket_text`. Using the old private name in the AC creates a terminology inconsistency that could mislead implementers into importing the private name.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '_ticket_text' with 'ticket_text' in the AC parenthetical.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `test_sentinel_mismatch` (or equivalent) to `tests/unit/test_ticket_digest_store.py` asserting that `latest_ticket_digest` returns `None` (or equivalent 'absent') when the stored event's `schema` field is a non-matching value (e.g., `ticket_digest_v2`, `plan_review_result_v1`, or absent key).
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `latest_ticket_digest` guards on the `ticket_digest_v1` sentinel so that a foreign/older-schema payload is skipped rather than mis-parsed, but no test exercises this sentinel-mismatch (foreign schema) failure path — e.g., a stored event whose `schema` field is `ticket_digest_v2`, missing, or otherwise wrong. All listed tests exercise only the happy-path (matching sentinel) or lower-level concerns (fsck, CLI); the mis-parse-prevention invariant is untested.
findings.location: Testing section / AC list — `latest_ticket_digest` sentinel guard; `tests/unit/test_ticket_digest_store.py`
findings.suggested_fix:
Add to `tests/unit/test_ticket_digest_store.py`:
```python
def test_sentinel_mismatch_returns_none(store, ticket_id):
    # Append a TICKET_DIGEST event with a wrong schema sentinel
    _raw_append(store, ticket_id, {"schema": "ticket_digest_v2", "digest": {}, "content_hash": "abc", "model": "x", "digest_hash_version": 1})
    result = latest_ticket_digest(ticket_id)
    assert result is None  # sentinel guard must skip, not mis-parse
```
And add a corresponding AC line: 'Foreign-schema sentinel → `latest_ticket_digest` returns None; `::test_sentinel_mismatch`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_store.py::test_event_types_all_listed_in_error_string` to the plan's list of tests that must pass (it will fail if `TICKET_DIGEST` is added to `EVENT_TYPES` but not to the hardcoded StoreError string literal at event_append.py:97-103).
findings.finding: The plan names adding `TICKET_DIGEST` to `EVENT_TYPES` and updating the StoreError hardcoded string at `event_append.py:97-103`, but does NOT enumerate the existing drift-guard test `tests/unit/test_store.py::test_event_types_all_listed_in_error_string` (line 76-83) — which asserts that EVERY name in `EVENT_TYPES` appears in that error string. An implementer who adds `TICKET_DIGEST` to `EVENT_TYPES` but fails to update the error string literal will silently break this existing test (it is in a separate test file from the new `test_ticket_digest_store.py`).
findings.location: Scope section / `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` edit target
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section for `event_append.py`, append: 'Note: the existing `tests/unit/test_store.py::test_event_types_all_listed_in_error_string` drift-guard will also pass only when both the frozenset and the StoreError string literal are updated together.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `latest_ticket_digest` resolves 'latest' via filename-prefix sort (mirroring `REVIEW_RESULT`'s `sidecar.py` exactly) or a distinct HLC-body comparison, and update the `test_latest_wins` description to match the chosen mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan's test `test_latest_wins` asserts 'two appends, latest-by-HLC', but the REVIEW_RESULT prior art (`sidecar.py::latest_review_result`) resolves 'latest' by lexicographic filename sort (`sorted(...files)[-1]`), not by reading an HLC field from event bodies. The plan never specifies whether `latest_ticket_digest` deviates from this prior-art mechanism or uses the same filename-sort approach under a different label — leaving an unverified capability assertion in the AC.
findings.location: Testing section / AC: `::test_latest_wins` (two appends, latest-by-HLC); compare with `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py::latest_review_result` (filename sort)
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt the prior-art filename-sort approach verbatim (it already encodes HLC ordering because `event_append` uses HLC for the timestamp prefix) and retitle the test `test_latest_wins_filename_sort` with a note that filename lexicographic order IS HLC order.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md entry capturing the TICKET_DIGEST sidecar pattern (sentinel guard, prune-on-write, shared-store-as-cache contract) so future LLM artifact types have a documented template to follow.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new pipeline stage (shared LLM-artifact cache via TICKET_DIGEST sidecar) and new cross-component dependencies, but does not mention updating any ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design document to capture this architectural decision.
findings.location: ## Scope (exact edit targets) / overall plan — no documentation update section present
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section '## Architecture note / ADR' to the plan (or reference an existing doc to update) covering: (1) sentinel-guard contract for all `*_v1` sidecar types, (2) prune-on-write keep=1 policy rationale, (3) shared store push as the cache-hit mechanism. This can be a CLAUDE.md append or an inline ADR in the repo.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify observability for `emit()`, `prune()`, and `latest_ticket_digest()` failure paths — at minimum a structured log line (with ticket_id and error) on any exception, so operators can detect when digest writes or reads are failing.
findings.finding: No observability specified for new failure points: emit() failures, prune() failures, and stale-digest observations are not instrumented with any log, metric, or trace. Operators cannot detect when digest writes are silently failing or when the shared store is degraded.
findings.location: New `src/rebar/llm/overlap/digest_sidecar.py` — emit(), prune(), latest_ticket_digest()
findings.suggested_fix: Add `except Exception: logger.warning('digest_sidecar.emit failed', ticket_id=ticket_id, exc_info=True)` (or equivalent) in emit() and prune(), and log at DEBUG when latest_ticket_digest returns None due to a read error vs. genuine absence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address concurrency safety of the append+prune pair in the shared store: either document that the store's HLC ordering makes the latest-wins prune safe under concurrent writers, or specify a coordination mechanism (e.g., advisory lock, idempotency key, or accept-duplicates-and-let-read-resolve strategy).
findings.finding: Concurrent prune race in the shared (auto-pushed) store: two clients enriching the same ticket simultaneously both call emit() → append → prune(keep=1). Each prune runs independently and may delete the other client's record, leaving 1 entry but with non-deterministic winner — or transiently 0 entries if both prunes execute between each other's appends. The plan names no lock, CAS, or coordination mechanism.
findings.location: Shared-state lifecycle — `emit()` + `prune(keep=1)` in digest_sidecar.py; Dependencies section ('shared store — auto-pushed')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `_cmd_show` catches exceptions from `latest_ticket_digest()` and degrades gracefully (e.g., renders `digest_freshness: "error"` or omits the field with a stderr warning) rather than propagating the error and crashing the show command.
findings.finding: The CLI `show` non-happy-path when `latest_ticket_digest()` itself raises (e.g., store I/O error mid-show) is unspecified: the plan does not say whether `_cmd_show` should degrade gracefully (e.g., treat as 'absent', display a warning) or propagate the exception and crash the show command.
findings.location: `src/rebar/_engine_support/reads_cli.py::_cmd_show` — freshness render section; Testing: `test_cmd_show_renders_freshness`
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the `latest_ticket_digest` call in a try/except in `_cmd_show`; on exception, set `digest_freshness = 'unavailable'` (or omit the key) and log a warning, so the rest of the ticket state is still displayed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `digest_freshness` output vocabulary as named constants (e.g. `FRESHNESS_FRESH`, `FRESHNESS_STALE`, `FRESHNESS_ABSENT`) in `digest_sidecar.py` and import them in `reads_cli.py` and the test file, so the three string values have a single source of truth.
findings.finding: The `digest_freshness` output values (`"present-fresh"`, `"present-stale"`, `"absent"`) are introduced as inline prose strings but are never consolidated as a named constant or exported enum in `digest_sidecar.py`. Since `reads_cli.py` emits them and tests assert them, both must independently hardcode the same string literals without a shared source of truth — a structural gap that causes silent divergence if one site is updated.
findings.location: Plan section: 'show freshness render' / `src/rebar/llm/overlap/digest_sidecar.py` (to be created)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `digest_sidecar.py`: `FRESHNESS_FRESH = "present-fresh"`, `FRESHNESS_STALE = "present-stale"`, `FRESHNESS_ABSENT = "absent"`. Import these in `reads_cli.py::_cmd_show` and in `test_cmd_show_renders_freshness`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the Scope section whether this story adds the `schemas/__init__.py` wiring (`TICKET_DIGEST` constant + `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS` entry) or gates it on S1 being merged first, and add a note to the Dependencies section or add `schemas/__init__.py` to this story's edit targets with the appropriate wiring.
findings.finding: The plan adds `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_digest.schema.json` (a new schema file on disk) but does NOT include `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` in this story's Scope edit targets. The `test_every_schema_file_is_wired` guard (confirmed at `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py:37`) asserts every `.schema.json` file is in `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`. The plan delegates the wiring to S1 (`OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["enrich"] = TICKET_DIGEST`) but this story's scope is silent on the inter-story ordering dependency — if this story is merged before S1, `test_every_schema_file_is_wired` will fail.
findings.location: Plan section: 'Scope (exact edit targets)' — `src/rebar/schemas/__init__.py` is absent
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'If S1 (ee3d) has not yet added `TICKET_DIGEST` to `schemas/__init__.py`, this story adds it: `TICKET_DIGEST = "ticket_digest"` to `__all__` and `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["enrich"] = TICKET_DIGEST` so `test_every_schema_file_is_wired` passes regardless of merge order.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
